body:
Title: CRA Form T777 2025 Statement of Employment Expenses.

Sources:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t777/t777-25e.pdf
- https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/publications/t4044/employment-expenses.html

CRA Form T777 2025 calculates total employment expenses for line 22900 of the T1 return. Line 4 multiplies food, beverages, and entertainment expenses by 50%. Line 9 caps tradesperson's tools expenses at $1,000. Line 15 adds accounting and legal fees, advertising and promotion, allowable motor vehicle expenses, food and entertainment expenses after the 50% limit, lodging, parking, office supplies, other employment expenses, tradesperson's tools expenses, apprentice mechanic tools expenses, labour mobility deduction, musical instrument expenses, capital cost allowance for musical instruments, and artists' employment expenses. Line 16 is work-space-in-the-home expenses. Line 17 adds line 15 and line 16 and is entered on T1 line 22900.

Line 3 calculates allowable motor vehicle expenses. Line 20 divides kilometres driven to earn employment income by total kilometres driven in the year. Line 29 adds fuel or electricity, maintenance and repairs, insurance, licence and registration, capital cost allowance, interest expense, leasing costs, and other vehicle expenses. Line 30 multiplies line 20 by line 29. Line 32 subtracts rebates, motor vehicle allowances, and reimbursements not included in income from line 30 and is entered on line 3 of page 1.

Line 11 calculates the labour mobility deduction for eligible tradespeople. Line 37 adds eligible temporary relocation expenses, prior-year carryforward from the same relocation, transportation expenses, meal expenses, and temporary lodging expenses. Line 38 multiplies eligible tradesperson employment income at the temporary work location by 50%. Line 39 is the least of line 37, line 38, the amount claimed for the relocation, and $4,000. Line 40 subtracts line 39 from line 37 and carries unused temporary relocation expenses forward. Line 41 totals line 39 amounts for all eligible temporary relocations and caps the total labour mobility deduction for the year at $4,000.

Line 16 calculates work-space-in-the-home expenses. Line 47 adds electricity, heat, water, home Internet access fees, maintenance, home insurance for commission employees, property taxes for commission employees, and other expenses such as rent. Line 50 adds the employment-use amount and prior-year carryforward. Line 52 enters line 15 and employment-related lines 20700 and 21200. Line 53 subtracts line 52 from employment income and floors at zero. Line 54 is the lesser of line 50 and line 53. Line 55 subtracts line 53 from line 50 and floors at zero as work-space-in-the-home expenses available to use in future years.

The capital cost allowance schedule gives Part A rates of 20% for Class 8, 30% for Class 10, 30% for Class 54, and 40% for Class 55. Part B gives a 30% rate for Class 10.1 passenger vehicles. Part A column 7 adds UCC at the start of the year and additions, then subtracts proceeds of dispositions. Column 8 subtracts additions from disposition proceeds and adds AIIP, RIIP, and ZEV additions, floored at zero. Column 9 applies the relevant factor to AIIP and ZEV additions after column 8. Column 10 subtracts column 4 from column 8 and floors at zero. Column 11 applies the relevant factor to RIIP and ZEV additions after column 10. Column 12 subtracts columns 4, 5, and 6 from column 3, divides by 2, and floors at zero. Column 13 adds columns 7, 9, and 11 and subtracts column 12. Column 15 multiplies column 13 by the CCA rate or a lower claimed amount. Column 16 subtracts column 15 from column 7. The stated 2025 relevant factors are 1.5 for Class 54 AIIP/ZEV acquired before 2025, 0.875 for Class 55 AIIP/ZEV acquired before 2025, 2.3333333333 for Class 54 RIIP/ZEV acquired after 2024, 1.5 for Class 55 RIIP/ZEV acquired after 2024, and 0.5 for Class 8 and Class 10 RIIP acquired after 2024.

Numeric grounding: T777 line 4 rate is 0.50. T777 line 9 cap is 1000 dollars. T777 line 38 rate is 0.50. T777 line 39 and line 41 cap is 4000 dollars. T777 Part A rates are 0.20, 0.30, 0.30, and 0.40. T777 Part B Class 10.1 rate is 0.30. T777 Part A factors are 1.5, 0.875, 2.3333333333, 1.5, and 0.50.
